strengths: Data modeling and data quality practices
weaknesses: No Pub/Sub or messaging system experience listed
No mention of MCP/LLM integrations or Anthropic/OpenAI use
improvement_bullets: Developed robust data ingestion workflows and backend services using FastAPI and Python to support real-time data processing and internal API integration.
Engineered scalable ETL pipelines using Airflow, with a rapid transition to Dagster, to process large-scale datasets while managing distributed workloads and message-based architectures like Pub/Sub.
Optimized data transformation and machine learning pipelines for demand forecasting, utilizing Dask for distributed computing to improve processing speed and resource efficiency.
Integrated LLM-driven agentic workflows using OpenAI APIs and MCP servers to automate internal reporting and provide actionable insights to non-technical stakeholders.
Managed infrastructure-as-code deployments using Terraform and Docker, ensuring consistent cloud environments for high-availability data services.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer opportunity with Guac. I am interested in this opportunity because of Guac’s innovative approach to solving the massive challenge of grocery food waste through AI-driven demand forecasting. What interests me about working for Guac is its mission to combine high-level technical solutions with tangible environmental and economic impact, which deeply intrigues me because of my own background in scaling data infrastructure and optimizing complex operations. I believe my expertise in building scalable ETL pipelines and my proficiency in Python-based backend development would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with an engineering-first mindset, focusing on reliability and data quality. In fact, at my previous role, I built a production-grade ELT pipeline ingesting large-scale economic datasets into Google BigQuery; I implemented rigorous data quality validation with Great Expectations and deployed a robust CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions. The result was a significantly streamlined data infrastructure that reduced query times and ensured 100% data integrity for stakeholders. Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer role at Guac would allow me to bring my experience with cloud-native data architecture to build pipelines that process billions of rows for grocery retailers. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my backend service skills. I collaborated with stakeholders to align software features with business processes, which led to a 15% increase in feature adoption by translating complex user feedback into clean, production-ready code. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python and distributed data systems in working on forecasting-ready data pipelines at Guac. I know that my proficiency in the modern data stack and my ability to navigate technical ambiguity align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
